titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Charles Scott Correspondence
titlestmt.author: Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday June 17, 2021 at 03:04

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract:
This collection consists chiefly of business correspondence to Charles Scott regarding the Oregon Land Co. and the Woodburn Milling Co., 1889-1909. Business correspondents include merchants, producers, manufacturers, traders, and individual landholders in Oregon, Washington, California, Montana, South Dakota, Pennsylvania, and Iowa.
Included is personal correspondence from Scott's family, as well as letters soliciting his endorsements for political candidates. Multiple letters concern the Oregon State Secular Union.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Michael Ginsburg Books on the Winlock William Miller, Jr. Memorial Fund, 2011.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Woodburn Milling Company, 1896-1899. II. Oregon Land Company, 1889-1902. III. Other Business and Personal Papers, 1897-1909.

bioghist.head: Charles Scott (1863-1923)
bioghist.p: Charles Scott (1863-1923) was born to Ann West Scott (1835-1909) and Robert Hall Scott (1826-1897) in Oregon.

In 1866, Robert Hall Scott purchased a flour mill and sawmill on Butte Creek in Marion County, Oregon, with his brother Thomas Scott--consequently, the village soon became known as Scotts Mills. Later, in 1897, Charles Scott, his father, and brother Robert Hall Scott (1875-1950) established the Woodburn Milling Co. in Woodburn, Oregon.
In 1916, Charles Scott served as the first mayor of Scotts Mills.
Charles Scott married Lulu A. Shaver Scott (1871-1956) in 1893. The couple had two sons, Alfred Merle Scott (1894-1977) and Charles W. Scott (1915- ).

bioghist.head: Oregon Land Company
bioghist.p: In 1888, the Oregon Land Co. organized with Henry John Minthorn (1846-1922) as its president. In 1889, the company bought around 2,200 acres of land surrounding Scotts Mills, Oregon, previously owned by Robert Hall Scott.
The company then marketed the land to Quakers primarily from Indiana, Ohio, and Pennsylvania as an offshoot from the Quaker settlement at Newberg, Oregon.
In 1892, the company organized a Friends Colony at Scotts Mills. By March 1898, it declared insolvency and made an assignment for the benefit of its creditors to Charles Wood.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Charles Scott Correspondence. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection consists chiefly of business correspondence to Charles Scott regarding the Oregon Land Co. and the Woodburn Milling Co., 1889-1909. Business correspondents include merchants, producers, manufacturers, traders, and individual landholders in Oregon, Washington, California, Montana, South Dakota, Pennsylvania, and Iowa.
Included is personal correspondence from Scott's family, as well as letters soliciting his endorsements for political candidates. Multiple letters concern the Oregon State Secular Union.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Charles Scott Correspondence is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is arranged alphabetically by correspondent.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I contains business correspondence to Charles Scott regarding the Woodburn Milling Co.

did.unittitle: Ames and Harris Bags, Portland, Oregon

Portland Flouring Mill Company, Portland, Oregon (35 folders)
Statesman Publishing Company, Salem, Oregon (2 folders)
W. C. Noon Bag Co., Portland, Oregon (3 folders)

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is arranged alphabetically by correspondent.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II contains business correspondence to Charles Scott regarding the Oregon Land Co.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series III is arranged alphabetically by correspondent.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III contains business and personal correspondence to Charles Scott.

did.unittitle: Catterlin, F. J., Salem, Oregon (2 folders)
Commons, F. E., Scotts Mills, Oregon (2 folders)
